title: cenchrus abyssinicus
intro: Cenchrus abyssinicus, also known as Dinkel or Abyssinian millet, is a type of drought-tolerant, annual grass belonging to the family Poaceae. Traditionally, Cenchrus abyssinicus has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and respiratory problems. The plant is rich in flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are believed to contribute to its health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. Cenchrus abyssinicus is often used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into traditional remedies and supplements.
